LAW OFFICES OF

GREVE, CLIFFORD, DIEPENBROCK & PARAS
1000 G STREET, SUITE 400
POST OFFICE BOX 2469

CLAIRE H. GREVE
ANDREW C. DIEPENBROCK
LAWRENCE A. WENCEL
WILLIAM C. BARRY
RICHARD L. MANFORD
DIANNA Z. HOFFMAN
E. EARL SECHRIST III
DENNIS W. RICHARDSON
SUSAN E. DOUGHERTY
JERILYN PAIK
MICHAEL E. BARBER
WILLIAM L. BAKER
PAUL C. KWONG
SCOTT E. COFER
THOMAS A. CREEGER
JOZEF BRUNETT
SUZANNE L. SMALL
MARGARET A. OLDHAM
BRIAN G. SOURLETI
F. CLINTON MURPHY
BOB W. HADLEY
CHRISTOPHER O'ROURKE
F. LANE FINCH, JR.
BRADLEY W. KRACFEL
REGINA A. FAVORS
ESTELA O. PINO
ASTRID GIRKO

EDWARD T. CLIFFORD
(1943)
GORDON W. COOK
THOMAS S. KNOX
GARY L. VINSON
LOUIS J. ANAPOLSKY
GARY SCOTT DECKER
BRADLEY R. LARSON
JAMES M. HICKEY
DANIEL P. COSTA
MAUREEN A. LENIHAN
ROBERT L. COLLINS
J. BRIAN PUTLER
JOHN W. MUNSELL, CFLS
KATHLEEN B. COOKE
W. GREGORY DAY
MARK H. HARRIS
PATRICIA M. KERN
ROBERT McEVILLY
ELIZABETH McDONALD
N. ERIN R. BREWER
STEPHEN J. NARDINE
PETER D. LEMMON
SHELLEY A. KESTER
JAMES M. GENTILE
LISA M. SARRO

SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95812-2469
(916) 443-2011
FAX (916) 441-7457

GEORGE E PARAS (RETIRED)

OF COUNSEL
IRVING H. PERLUSS
PHILIP N. KNOX, JR.
MATTHEW V. BRADY

GERALD R. JOHNSON (1897-1973)

1750 MONTGOMERY STREET
SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 94111
(415) 543-6564

870 MANZANITA COURT
POST OFFICE BOX 3590
CHICO, CALIFORNIA 95927-3590
(916) 893-0000

April 17, 1990

Ms. Margaret Ellison
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95801-0807

RE: California-Nevada Super Speed Ground
Transportation Commission

Dear Margaret:

This letter follows up on our recent telephone conversation
regarding whether members of the California-Nevada Super Speed
Ground Transportation Commission ("The Commission") are obligated to
report as individual income or gifts the payment of certain
Commission expenses by third parties.

BACKGROUND

In late July and early August of 1989 members of the Commission
traveled to Germany, France and England to inspect other high speed
train systems. Funding for portions of this trip was provided by
the following: Transrapid International, which is constructing a
high speed train in Germany; Bechtel International, Inc., which has
formed a consortium with Transrapid to bid on the California-Nevada
project; the Government of France; and Alsthom, a French
manufacturer which also hopes to bid on the project.

GREVE, CLIFFORD, DIEPENBROCK & PARAS

Ms. Margaret Ellison
Fair Political Practices Commission
April 17, 1990
Page 2

     Transrapid had donated funds to the City of Las Vegas several
years ago for use in developing a high speed rail system. The City
of Las Vegas used those funds to pay certain start-up costs for the
Commission. Once the Commission was established, the City of Las
Vegas made a gift of those funds to the Commission.

     At its meeting of June 17, 1989 the Commission adopted a
resolution designed to comply with the FPPC's opinion in In re Peter
G. Stone, 3 FPPC Opinions 55. Thereafter, the Commission used some
of those funds to pay some of the expenses of the Commissioners
traveling in Germany.

     The Commission adopted an identical resolution in connection
with a gift from Alsthom and the Government of France. Alsthom and
the French Government, however, did not pay the money directly to
the Commission. Rather, they paid expenses incurred by the
Commission in France, including the cost of hotels, tours, meals and
ground transportation.

     Finally, Bechtel International, which is joined in a consortium
with Transrapid, paid the costs of hotels, meals and ground
transportation in England. Bechtel's contribution was not the
subject of a specific Commission resolution, although, as I have
noted above, Bechtel is involved in a joint venture with Transrapid
on this project.

                              ISSUES

     The questions which I posed to you are these:

     1.    Which, if any, of these payments are reportable
           by the Commissioners as individual income or
           gifts?

     2.    Assuming that some or all of them are
           reportable, must disclosure statements be filed
           by all of the California Commissioners or
           merely the California Commissioners who are
           elected officials?

     With respect to the second question, you will recall our
earlier discussions in which I noted that the eight California
Commissioners include some elected officials at both the state and
local level, one Commissioner who is also an appointee to the State
Transportation Commission and three individuals from the private
sector who hold no public office apart from their membership on the
Commission. You and I have also discussed the fact that the
Commission has not yet adopted a conflict of interest code and
probably will not do so until its meeting in May.

GREVE, CLIFFORD, DIEPENBROCK & PARAS

Ms. Margaret Ellison
Fair Political Practices Commission
April 17, 1990
Page 3

     I would appreciate a response by telephone, followed up by a
letter opinion.  The Commission meets Friday, April 20 in Ontario.

     Thank you as always for your prompt attention.

                                        Yours sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Thomas S. Knox


TASK/lhf

sections.facts:
In late July and early August of 1989 members of the Commission traveled to Germany, France and England to inspect other high speed train systems. Funding for portions of this trip was provided by the following: Transrapid International, which is constructing a high speed train in Germany; Bechtel International, Inc., which has formed a consortium with Transrapid to bid on the California-Nevada project; the Government of France; and Alsthom, a French manufacturer which also hopes to bid on the project.

Ms. Margaret Ellison  
Fair Political Practices Commission  
April 17, 1990

Transrapid had donated funds to the City of Las Vegas several years ago for use in developing a high speed rail system. The City of Las Vegas used those funds to pay certain start-up costs for the Commission. Once the Commission was established, the City of Las Vegas made a gift of those funds to the Commission.

At its meeting of June 17, 1989 the Commission adopted a resolution designed to comply with the FPPC's opinion in In re Peter G. Stone, 3 FPPC Opinions 55. Thereafter, the Commission used some of those funds to pay some of the expenses of the Commissioners traveling in Germany.

The Commission adopted an identical resolution in connection with a gift from Alsthom and the Government of France. Alsthom and the French Government, however, did not pay the money directly to the Commission. Rather, they paid expenses incurred by the Commission in France, including the cost of hotels, tours, meals and ground transportation.

Finally, Bechtel International, which is joined in a consortium with Transrapid, paid the costs of hotels, meals and ground transportation in England. Bechtel's contribution was not the subject of a specific Commission resolution, although, as I have noted above, Bechtel is involved in a joint venture with Transrapid on this project.

ISSUES

The questions which I posed to you are these:

1. Which, if any, of these payments are reportable by the Commissioners as individual income or gifts?

2. Assuming that some or all of them are reportable, must disclosure statements be filed by all of the California Commissioners or merely the California Commissioners who are elected officials?

With respect to the second question, you will recall our earlier discussions in which I noted that the eight California Commissioners include some elected officials at both the state and local level, one Commissioner who is also an appointee to the State Transportation Commission and three individuals from the private sector who hold no public office apart from their membership on the Commission. You and I have also discussed the fact that the Commission has not yet adopted a conflict of interest code and probably will not do so until its meeting in May.

GREVE, CLIFFORD, DIEPENBROCK & PARAS

Ms. Margaret Ellison
Fair Political Practices Commission
April 17, 1990

I would appreciate a response by telephone, followed up by a letter opinion. The Commission meets Friday, April 20 in Ontario.

Thank you as always for your prompt attention.

Yours sincerely,

Thomas S. Knox
